name: Valle del Río Corb — circular por Albió, Segura y Vallfogona del Riu Corb
region_intro: Hiking around Vallfogona de Riucorb offers diverse natural landscapes within the Conca de Barberà region of central Catalonia. The area is characterized by the Corb River valley, featuring agricultural lands with olive groves, orchards, and vineyards. To the south and southeast, the Comalats range provides extensive pine and oak forests. This blend of riverine and forested terrain creates varied hiking opportunities. Best hiking trails around Vallfogona De Riucorb The most popular hiking route is Cercavins Route – hiking in Catalonia, a 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the local countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Valle del Río Corb — circular por Albió, Segura y Vallfogona del Riu Corb, a moderate 9.3 miles (14.9 km) path. This circular route traverses the Corb River valley, passing through several villages. Local hikers also love the Countryside Around Albió – Albió loop from Albió, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Hiking around Vallfogona de Riucorb is defined by the Corb River valley, the Comalats range, and extensive pine and oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more moderate excursions. The routes in Vallfogona de Riucorb are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore Vallfogona de Riucorb's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Vallfogona de Riucorb?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails in the Vallfogona de Riucorb area, offering a diverse range of experiences. Most routes are moderate, with several easy options and a few more challenging excursions.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hikes in this region?
A: The majority of hikes around Vallfogona de Riucorb are classified as moderate, making them suitable for those with a good basic fitness level. There are also several easy strolls, perfect for a relaxed outing, and a couple of more difficult routes for experienced hikers seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Vallfogona de Riucorb?
A: Yes, the region offers several easy trails suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31827148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Countryside Around Albió – Albió loop from Albió&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6 km route through agricultural landscapes, which can be completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes, making it a great choice for a family outing.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Circular routes are a popular way to explore the region. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1134485470/valle-del-rio-corb-circular-por-albio-segura-y-vallfogona-del-riu-corb" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Río Corb — circular por Albió, Segura y Vallfogona del Riu Corb&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 14.9 km path that traverses the scenic Corb River valley and passes through charming villages.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks or scenery can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Hiking in Vallfogona de Riucorb offers a blend of landscapes. You'll experience the rich agricultural tapestry of the Corb River valley with its olive groves, orchards, and vineyards, especially vibrant in spring. To the south and southeast, trails lead through extensive pine and oak forests within the Comalats range. The area is part of the Espai Natural de les Obagues del Riu Corb, highlighting its preserved natural environment.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions to visit while hiking?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. Vallfogona de Riucorb itself retains medieval character with its castle and the parish church of Santa Maria. Many routes pass through or near charming medieval towns in the wider Vall del Corb, such as Guimerà. You can also find historical landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8049582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guimerà Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5030088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Tallat&lt;/a&gt;, which offers magnificent panoramic views.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Vallfogona de Riucorb?
A: The trails around Vallfogona de Riucorb are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Hikers often praise the diverse natural scenery, the tranquility of the Corb River valley, and the opportunity to explore historical villages and cultural routes like the Cistercian Route.

Q: Is there public transport or parking available for hikers?
A: While specific public transport options to trailheads might be limited, Vallfogona de Riucorb village itself serves as a starting point for several routes, including those leading to Font d'en Ballart and Sant Pere dels Bigats. Parking is generally available within the village, making it a convenient base for exploring the surrounding trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Vallfogona de Riucorb?
A: Spring is particularly beautiful for hiking in Vallfogona de Riucorb, as the Corb Valley transforms into a vibrant, flowery landscape with blooming poppies, roses, and almond trees. Autumn also offers pleasant temperatures and colorful scenery. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winter provides a quieter, often crisp experience.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, many routes offer excellent viewpoints. For instance, the Sanctuaries of La Bovera de Guimerà and Tallat de Rocallaura, accessible via some trails, provide magnificent panoramic views of the Cistercian territory. Additionally, you can find specific viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072094" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Albió&lt;/a&gt; along certain paths.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through vineyards or agricultural areas?
A: Many trails in the Corb River valley wind through the region's rich agricultural landscapes. You'll frequently encounter olive groves, orchards, vineyards, and almond trees. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200082607/cercavins-route-hiking-in-catalonia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cercavins Route – hiking in Catalonia&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, exploring the local countryside and its agricultural heritage.
tips: Albió is a population center located at the westernmost end of the term of Llorac, in the Conca de Barberà. It is located on a small tossal.

Albió is a town belonging to the municipality of Llorac, in the Conca de Barberà. In the town, the remains of a medieval castle, a 13th century mill and the Romanesque church of Sant Gil stand out. In the town you can find several rural tourism and an artisan cheese shop.

Segura is a town belonging to the municipality of Savallà del Comtat, in the Conca de Barberà region. The town has traces of a medieval castle, a baroque church and lovely views of the surrounding fields and hills.
